items.title: Government Bans Anthropic AI Model Over Cybersecurity Threat Concerns
items.summary: Following Anthropic's creation of its coding-capable AI model, Mythos, and subsequent public release of a modified version named Fable, the federal government placed export controls on it. Hours after these actions, Anthropic revoked access to both AI models. The incident has spurred calls in Europe for increased domestic AI development but is complicated by China's highly capable and accessible open-source models. These developments suggest companies might pivot toward Chinese alternatives to avoid US or European restrictions.
items.audio_script: From technologyreview.com, "Government Bans Anthropic AI Model Over Cybersecurity Threat Concerns." The story details federal government actions regarding coding-capable artificial intelligence models and the subsequent removal of access by the developing company. After the public release of a modified model called Fable, which followed the creation of the initial coding-AI model named Mythos, the federal government imposed export controls on the technology. In response to these measures, Anthropic subsequently revoked all user access to both AI models. This sequence of events has spurred increased advocacy for domestic artificial intelligence development within Europe, a push complicated by the robust and readily available open-source models emanating from China. Industry attention may now shift toward Chinese alternatives to navigate existing US and European regulatory restrictions.

items.title: Mistral OCR 4 Brings Citation-Ready Structured Output to RAG, Agentic, and Enterprise Search Pipelines
items.summary: Mistral AI released OCR 4 on June 23, 2026, enhancing document processing beyond simple text extraction. The new model provides structured output for each block, including a bounding box, typed classification, and confidence scores at both page and word levels. It supports a wide range of 170 languages. Functioning within a single self-hosted container, OCR 4 integrates into RAG, agentic, and enterprise search workflows via one API endpoint. This capability delivers citation-ready data for advanced AI applications.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Mistral OCR 4 Brings Citation-Ready Structured Output to RAG, Agentic, and Enterprise Search Pipelines." This report details the release of Mistral's updated optical character recognition model, which significantly enhances document processing by providing deeply structured data extraction. The new iteration offers granular output for every text block encountered in a document, including precise bounding boxes, assigned type classifications, and confidence scoring reported at both the page and word levels. Furthermore, the system boasts support across a diverse catalog of 170 languages and operates entirely within a single self-hosted container framework accessible through one unified API endpoint. This advancement directly enables the generation of citation-ready data streams for sophisticated artificial intelligence applications involving retrieval-augmented generation, autonomous agents, and large-scale enterprise search initiatives. Industry adoption is expected to accelerate the reliability and precision of document interaction pipelines across various commercial sectors.

items.title: Datalab Releases lift: A 9B Open-Weights Vision Model That Extracts Structured JSON From PDFs Using Schemas
items.summary: Datalab unveiled "lift," a new 9B open-weights vision model. This model specializes in converting content from PDFs and images into structured, schema-matching JSON format. It employs schema-constrained decoding to ensure output validity and utilizes trained abstention to accurately return null for missing fields. Benchmark testing demonstrated its high performance, achieving 90.2% field accuracy across a set of 225 documents.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Datalab Releases lift: A 9B Open-Weights Vision Model That Extracts Structured JSON From PDFs Using Schemas." The announcement details a new model designed for converting visual and document data into highly structured digital formats. Datalab introduced lift, an open-weights vision model with nine billion parameters, engineered specifically to transform content found within PDF documents and images. The system operates using schema-constrained decoding, ensuring the resulting JSON output rigorously adheres to predefined structural rules, while employing trained abstention methods to accurately report null values for any data fields that are absent from the source material. Benchmarking results indicated a strong performance level, achieving ninety point two percent field accuracy across an evaluation set comprising two hundred and twenty-five distinct documents. This release marks a significant advancement in automated data extraction capabilities within the artificial intelligence domain.

Implementation of this method achieves an observed increase in inference throughput by up to fifteen times when run on NVIDIA Blackwell hardware. items.title: Giant Chip Machine Powers Future of AI Computing
items.summary: ASML's executive vice president of technology is showcasing a massive lithography machine crucial for advanced microchip production. This 150-ton apparatus utilizes extreme-ultraviolet (EUV) light generated by lasers to pattern transistor features on silicon wafers. The new machines can achieve an unprecedented resolution of eight nanometers, allowing for denser circuitry in powerful chips. These state-of-the-art devices are reaching chipmaking factories at a cost of $400 million each. Such technology is vital for sustaining Moore's Law and meeting the ravenous computing demands of the growing AI industry.
items.audio_script: From technologyreview.com, "Giant Chip Machine Powers Future of AI Computing." The report details a massive lithography apparatus critical for advancing microchip manufacturing capabilities. An ASML executive vice president of technology presented the machine, which weighs 150 tons and employs extreme-ultraviolet or EUV light generated by lasers to etch transistor features onto silicon wafers. These advanced instruments are engineered to achieve an unprecedented resolution measuring eight nanometers, facilitating more complex circuitry within powerful microchips, with each unit arriving at fabrication plants priced near $400 million. This technology is considered essential for sustaining Moore's Law and meeting the substantial computational demands presented by the expanding artificial intelligence sector.

items.title: [AINews] Claude Tag: Multiplayer, Proactive, Persistent Agents in Slack

These capabilities establish Tag as a significant advancement signaling the future of automated work processes.

items.title: Retrieval Is Filtering, Not Search: A Mental Model for Enterprise RAG
items.summary: Suggests a paradigm shift in understanding Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for enterprise use cases. Advocates viewing retrieval as a filtering process rather than traditional searching by mere string matching. Recommends developers focus on techniques like "filtering line\_df and toc\_df" within document intelligence workflows. Highlights the strategy of using small anchors to expand context, improving accuracy. This model provides a necessary conceptual framework for building robust enterprise RAG solutions.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "Retrieval Is Filtering, Not Search: A Mental Model for Enterprise RAG." The article proposes a significant conceptual shift regarding the implementation of Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems in corporate environments. It suggests that developers should reframe how these systems operate by viewing retrieval as a filtering mechanism rather than one reliant on simple string matching techniques common to traditional search engines. This perspective advises focusing development efforts on methods such as programmatically filtering dataframes within document intelligence pipelines and employing smaller, strategic anchors to augment the provided context for enhanced accuracy in outputs. This revised model offers a necessary conceptual foundation for constructing more dependable and robust RAG solutions across large organizations.

items.title: Anchor Detection for RAG: Parallel Detectors, Then One LLM Call at the End
items.summary: A new approach for Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) involves implementing anchor detection using parallel detectors. These specialized detectors are employed to gather information before a final Large Language Model (LLM) call is made. This method aims to improve RAG performance by structuring the information retrieval process. The technique suggests integrating multiple specialized detectors rather than relying on sequential filtering methods. Ultimately, it streamlines the architecture to finish with a single, powerful LLM prompt.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "Anchor Detection for RAG: Parallel Detectors, Then One LLM Call at the End." This discussion presents a novel methodology designed to enhance Retrieval Augmented Generation performance by overhauling the information retrieval structure. The approach details the implementation of anchor detection using multiple parallel detectors that gather necessary data points concurrently. Instead of employing sequential filtering mechanisms, this method integrates several specialized detectors upfront. The process culminates in submitting all gathered context through a single Large Language Model call for final generation. This architectural shift suggests a more robust and efficient design paradigm for complex RAG systems moving forward.

items.title: AI Distillation Moves Beyond Scale to Practical Intelligence
items.summary: Modern AI progress was heavily defined by sheer model scale, leading to massive, powerful frontier models capable of diverse tasks. However, these large models are costly, slow, and impractical for many real-world, niche applications. Distillation techniques are positioned as the solution, moving focus beyond mere size to practical intelligence. This approach aims to create smaller, more specialized, and efficient AI systems suitable for specific enterprise or local use cases. The goal is achieving reliable, repeatable competence rather than just generic, all-encompassing brilliance.
items.audio_script: From thesequence.substack.com, "AI Distillation Moves Beyond Scale to Practical Intelligence." This reporting details a current shift in artificial intelligence development away from maximizing model size toward achieving specialized operational capability through distillation techniques. The material explains that while frontier models have previously driven progress with immense scale and diverse capabilities, their high cost and computational demands limit real-world deployment for many niche applications. Distillation methodologies are presented as the solution by focusing on training smaller, more targeted AI systems to retain core functional intelligence from much larger predecessors. This marks a directional change emphasizing reliable competence over sheer general brilliance. The industry focus is expected to increasingly pivot toward efficiency metrics and task-specific deployments utilizing these distilled models.

items.title: FOD#157: What People Still Don’t Understand About AI Agents
items.summary: Answering questions at a BIO AI Summit revealed widespread misunderstanding regarding AI agents and tools. Specifically, there is confusion about whether equipping an agent with scientific toolkits provides dangerous new knowledge. items.audio_script: From MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech, "All challenges big and small." The report details an account of early technological deployment in a severely damaged urban environment. In 1991, upon reaching Kuwait shortly after the conclusion of the first Gulf War, observations focused on the operational realities faced by nascent infrastructure in areas suffering from intense disruption. Conditions included limited electrical availability necessitating reliance on generators and pervasive hazards such as scattered debris alongside unexploded ordnance, all set against a backdrop punctuated by significant oil fires illuminating the surrounding arid terrain. This narrative suggests that advanced technologies must be designed for robust function under conditions of extreme resource scarcity and environmental instability.

items.title: Learn more about our updated Terms of Service
items.summary: The updated Terms of Service will take effect on July 30, 2026, and clarify user expectations regarding the services. Changes include a new section addressing background internet usage and updates to dispute settlement procedures. For EEA residents, changes incorporate current local laws, including a withdrawal button. Users outside the EEA may see added disclosures, such as those related to AI outputs. If users disagree with the terms, they must remove their content and discontinue service use.
items.audio_script: From Google, "Learn more about our updated Terms of Service." The update establishes revised user expectations across Google's services, effective July 30, 2026. Key changes include a dedicated section addressing background internet usage and revisions to existing dispute settlement procedures. For residents within the EEA, the modifications integrate current local legislation, notably implementing a withdrawal button for users. Users located outside the EEA can expect additional disclosures concerning areas like artificial intelligence outputs. Should any user disagree with the updated terms, continued service use necessitates the removal of all associated content. These revisions signal an industry trend toward heightened regional compliance and granular disclosure within major tech platforms.

items.title: RIFT-Bench: Dynamic Red-teaming For Agentic AI Systems
items.summary: Introducing RIFT-Bench, a methodology designed for dynamic red-teaming of autonomous, large language model (LLM) powered agentic AI systems. It addresses limitations in current security evaluations by providing unified testing across diverse and heterogeneous architectures. The framework operates through two automated phases: Discovery, which maps the system's structure, and Scanning, which executes adaptive adversarial attacks. RIFT-Bench evaluates the target system itself using a wide variety of dynamically adaptable probes targeting multiple attack vectors. Demonstrating its effectiveness across 45 different agentic systems, it also supports direct assessment of existing mitigation strategies.

items.title: Neuro-Symbolic Drive: Rule-Grounded Faithful Reasoning for Driving VLAs
items.summary: Neuro-Symbolic Drive is a new framework that improves Vision-Language Action (VLA) models by grounding their reasoning in explicit rules. It supervises the VLA using structured, rule-grounded reasoning traces extracted from classical rule-based planners. These traces detail the planner's internal decisions and are directly coupled to the executed trajectory during fine-tuning of Qwen3.5-4B. By deriving supervision this way, it ensures causal connectivity between the reasoning steps and the resulting motion planning. On benchmarks, this method significantly reduced error rates, such as decreasing ADE@3s from 0.47 to 0.26 with three cameras.

items.title: Critique of Agent Model
items.summary: The paper examines the philosophical and technical boundaries between automation and genuine agency in modern AI systems like LLM "coding agents." It analyzes current AI agent architectures across five dimensions: goal, identity, decision-making, self-regulation, and learning. The authors argue that true agency requires these structures to be intrinsically internalized within the system, rather than relying solely on external engineering scaffolds. This distinction separates mere "agentic" tools from truly autonomous "agentive" systems capable of open-world operation. To address this gap, a proposed Goal-Identity-Configurator (GIC) architecture integrates goal decomposition, identity evolution, and world model simulation for general-purpose agency.

items.title: Safe and Generalizable Hierarchical Multi-Agent RL via Constraint Manifold Control
items.summary: A hierarchical multi-agent reinforcement learning framework addresses the trade-off between strong empirical performance and theoretical safety guarantees in safety-critical systems. It enforces hard safety constraints at a low level using a constraint manifold, even under mild assumptions. High-level policy learning manages effective coordination among multiple agents. The proposed method offers theoretical safety guarantees for the multi-agent setting and ensures stationary learning dynamics for stable training. Empirically, it shows competitive performance with near-perfect safety rates and good generalization across different agent numbers and obstacles.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning Towards Broadly and Persistently Beneficial Models
items.summary: As AI deployment increases in diverse, high-stakes settings, model alignment must generalize beyond training data, especially for reinforcement learning (RL) which risks misalignment via reward hacking. Researchers built a dataset spanning varied domains to train beneficial traits like truthfulness and fairness using RL. Training models on this dataset improved performance on over 80% of independent out-of-distribution alignment benchmarks compared to baselines. Notably, training limited to one domain, such as health, provided broad improvements in non-health areas by mitigating hacking and deception. Furthermore, beneficial trait RL enhanced alignment persistence, showing greater resistance against adversarial prompts and harmful fine-tuning attempts.

items.title: Can Language Model Agents be Helpful Circuit Explainers in Mechanistic Interpretability?
items.summary: Mechanistic interpretability has advanced in locating circuits, yet explaining their function is challenging. Researchers introduced AgenticInterpBench, a benchmark using 84 synthetic transformer circuits for component annotation. They propose HyVE, an agentic explainer that iteratively hypothesizes and validates circuit components through observation. Testing showed HyVE recovers useful explanations across various large language model backbones, although performance varies. Ultimately, while LM agents show promise, ensuring reliable validation remains the primary hurdle for automated circuit explanation.

items.title: Breaking the Filter Bubble: A Semantic Pareto-DQN Framework for Multi-Objective Recommendation
items.summary: Recommender systems often trap users in "filter bubbles" by optimizing solely for immediate engagement. Traditional single-objective models cannot balance this against crucial values like information diversity and provider fairness. The proposed solution is a multi-objective reinforcement learning framework treating recommendations as a semantic Markov decision process. Utilizing a Pareto-DQN agent, the system manages engagement, diversity, and fairness as separate reward signals, preventing simple scalarization. Experiments demonstrate that this method improves societal objectives while maintaining minimal impact on user engagement by mapping the Pareto frontier.

items.title: Ensemble Feature Selection and Harris Hawks Optimization for Explainable Mental Health Risk Prediction in Female Sex Workers
items.summary: Mental disorders, particularly depression, pose significant risks to female sex workers due to violence, stigma, and economic hardship. Recognizing the limitations of current machine learning models for this complex population, a hybrid predictive model was proposed. This model combines an ensemble feature selection approach (using ANOVA and mutual information) with Harris Hawks optimization-tuned logistic regression. When tested on 3,005 female sex workers, the system achieved high performance metrics: 95.78% accuracy, 95.77% F1 score, and 0.96 AUC, outperforming traditional classifiers. The resulting explainable AI tool identifies key contributors to depression, such as PTSD, client-related violence, and occupational factors, facilitating early, evidence-based care.

items.title: Beyond Trajectory Imitation: Strategy-Guided Policy Optimization for LLM Reasoning
items.summary: Current methods for transferring reasoning skills often rely on imitating specific solution steps, which leads to memorization rather than generalizable problem-solving. Strategy-Guided Policy Optimization (SGPO) overcomes this by distilling reusable strategy descriptions instead of entire answer trajectories. SGPO constructs both autonomous and strategically guided paths for comparison, using a token-level forward-KL objective for selective knowledge transfer. The framework employs adaptive instance weighting to guide learning when necessary and reduce it as the model improves. Experiments on mathematical benchmarks show SGPO significantly outperforms existing baselines across different model types.

items.title: Exploring Academic Influence of Algorithms by Co-occurrence Network Based on Full-text of Academic Papers
items.summary: Algorithms are central to contemporary scientific research within AI, but existing studies often overlook their collective influence through interconnections. This study constructs large-scale co-occurrence networks by analyzing the full text of academic papers in natural language processing (NLP). Deep learning models were used to extract algorithm entities and build cumulative, overall, and annual co-occurrence networks. Analyzing these structures reveals that algorithm networks exhibit complex network features, with connections becoming denser over two decades. The research demonstrates that highly influential algorithms maintain strong core positions within the network structure across time.

items.title: Systematic Exploration of 4-Expert Heterogeneous Mixture-of-Experts via Automated Pipeline Search
items.summary: An automated search pipeline was developed to systematically explore heterogeneous 4-Expert Mixture-of-Experts (MoE4) architectures using the LEMUR dataset. This generator combines base architecture families into ensembles controlled by a convolutional gating network, utilizing temperature scaling and mixup augmentation. Over a 28-day campaign, the pipeline generated 1,021 testable candidate models across 4,463 total combinations. A significant finding revealed that due to search method limitations, nearly the entire explored space was biased toward the AirNet family. Within this limited scope, ShuffleNet and MobileNetV3 consistently produced the highest accuracy ensembles.

items.title: Weight-Space Geometry of Offline Reasoning Training
items.summary: Comparing weight updates from six offline reasoning training methods (including SFT, RFT, DFT, RIFT, Offline GRPO, and DPO) using identical math rollouts reveals distinct geometric differences. SFT, RFT, and RIFT exhibit highly colinear weight deltas with similar accuracy on GSM8K. Conversely, DFT diverges significantly from reward-weighted methods, while Offline GRPO adds a large component orthogonal to the SFT direction. Notably, DPO achieves the highest reported accuracies on both GSM8K and AIME26 within this protocol, despite occupying a near-orthogonal subspace.

items.title: A Survey on Federated Causal Discovery and Inference
items.summary: Causal reasoning is crucial for data-driven decisions but faces challenges when data are distributed due to privacy or logistical constraints. Federated learning (FL) addresses this by enabling collaborative analysis without centralizing raw data, leading to federated causal discovery (FCD) and inference (FCI). This survey provides a comprehensive review of the field using multi-dimensional taxonomies organized by structural learning approach, data partitioning, and knowledge sharing. It details FCD methods across methodological paradigms and federation topologies, while categorizing FCI based on target estimands and estimation strategies. Finally, it unifies FCD and FCI, positioning them as sequential stages in a complete federated causal reasoning pipeline that shares concerns regarding privacy and efficiency.

items.title: Low-power analogue neural networks with trainable nonlinear connections for continuous control
items.summary: Physical neural networks aim for low-power machine learning by integrating computation with analogue device physics. This research proposes placing trainable nonlinear functions directly onto physical connections, moving beyond architectures where only scalar weights are supported. By implementing these functions using analogue band-pass filters on programmable arrays, the resulting networks excel at representing smooth, continuously valued targets like robotic kinematics and continuous control. These specialized networks require significantly fewer nodes and connections compared to multilayer perceptrons for such tasks. A dedicated CMOS implementation is projected to achieve power consumption around 30 microwatts, confirming that trainable connection nonlinearity provides the primary advantage.

items.title: Synergizing Physically Constrained MCMC and Chemical-Informed Gaussian Processes for Reaction Network Discovery
items.summary: A new gray-box workflow, PC-MCMC-CIGP, addresses the challenge of extracting governing equations from noisy chemical time-series data by combining multiple methods. This technique integrates spike-and-slab topology sampling and hard conservation principles with a Chemical-Informed Gaussian Process (CIGP) for parameter calibration. The core contribution lies in coupling these components into a physically constrained workflow featuring explicit uncertainty-aware acquisition choices. On the $\text{H}_2 + \text{Br}_2$ benchmark, the method successfully distinguishes true elementary reaction pathways from misleading phenomenological models. Furthermore, applying this to styrene epoxidation improved final yield by 12.5% compared to established Gaussian Process optimization baselines.

items.title: Exploring Dualistic Meta-Learning to Enhance Domain Generalization in Open Set Scenarios
items.summary: Domain generalization struggles with label mismatch when dealing with unseen target domains. Open set domain generalization aims to recognize novel, unobserved classes within these new environments. Traditional one-vs-all classifiers suffer from decision boundary skew due to sample imbalance, causing over-rejection of out-of-distribution data. To address this, the paper proposes dualistic Meta-Learning with joint Domain-Class matching (MEDIC). MEDIC optimizes boundaries by simultaneously considering implicit gradient matching across inter-domain and inter-class task splits.

items.title: One Ruler: A Same-Hands Re-Evaluation of Bivariate Causal Direction on Tuebingen, with a Parameter-Free Compression Baseline
items.summary: Researchers re-evaluated bivariate causal direction methods on identical datasets using a "same-hands" protocol with no tuning allowed. A minimal, parameter-free baseline involving sorted-conditional compression achieved 74.7% weighted accuracy. Performance comparisons showed that published figures for advanced methods like SLOPE and RECI often relied on biased or inconsistent evaluation protocols. Under this common ruler, high-performing methods clustered in the low-to-mid 70s, with the simple compressor achieving comparable results to the best reported scores. The study details mechanisms inflating published metrics, such as test-set model selection, and provides corrected benchmarks for established causal inference techniques.

items.title: Deciphering Fingerprints of 3D Molecular Surfaces for Accurate Epitope Prediction
items.summary: Molecular surfaces contain geometric and physicochemical patterns vital for antibody-antigen recognition and epitope prediction. Current methods often fail to model discontinuous epitopes because they rely only on sequences or backbone structures. SurfBind introduces a novel, surface-centric learning framework that operates directly on molecular surface representations. It integrates diverse cues using a Transformer architecture featuring patch-level modeling and binder-aware cross-attention. Testing on established benchmarks like SAbDab and DB5.5 confirms SurfBind achieves state-of-the-art performance across different antibody types and conformational states.

items.title: Reconstructing GRACE Terrestrial Water Storage with Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks: An Application to South America
items.summary: Terrestrial water storage (TWS) change, critical for understanding the global water cycle, has been observed by GRACE satellites since 2002. To extend this record, researchers developed a novel approach using a multi-variate time series Graph Neural Network (MTGNN). This model reconstructs monthly TWS anomalies back to 1940 by learning from daily ERA5 meteorological data and existing GRACE observations. The MTGNN incorporates spatial dependencies via an adjacency matrix combining proximity and lagged climatic correlations. Evaluation showed the graph-based model achieved a high basin-mean Pearson correlation of 0.94, proving statistically competitive with fewer required predictors than prior methods.

items.title: The Degeneracy Distillery
items.summary: Degeneracies occur when multiple parameters or labels result in similar data, complicating machine learning and inverse problems. The degeneracy distillery is a new method that detects and resolves these parameter degeneracies automatically and symbolically using only parameter-data pairs. It characterizes degeneracies as an inherent property of the physical model by analyzing the information geometry of the likelihood. The technique estimates and flattens the Fisher information matrix globally, unlike posterior methods that flatten only at one point. This results in discovering symbolic coordinate transformations that reduce simulation needs for neural posterior estimation by up to tenfold while providing deeper physical insights.

items.title: Automated Residual Plot Assessment With the R Package autovi and the Shiny Application autovi.web
items.summary: Visual assessment of residual plots for diagnosing linear models is currently subjective and difficult to scale manually. A proposed solution involves automating this process using a new R package that incorporates a computer vision model. This model evaluates the residuals by predicting a Visual Signal Strength (VSS). The development also includes an accompanying Shiny application to ensure user-friendliness. Ultimately, this tool provides analysts with quantitative support for assessing overall model fit.

items.title: Model selection with proper scoring rules on data sets of time series
items.summary: Addresses model selection among probabilistic time series models by utilizing proper scoring rules to generate an average score for each individual time series. Previous methods often use summary statistics like mean, median, and mean rank, which can yield conflicting model selection decisions due to the skewness of the score distribution. The study demonstrates that as the test set size increases, these diverse criteria converge on the same conclusion. Specifically for short test sets, only the mean score reliably identifies the true underlying model. Analysis using intermittent time series confirms this convergence trend and highlights the necessity of large test sets.

items.title: The Degeneracy Distillery
items.summary: Degeneracies occur when multiple parameters or labels yield similar data, complicating machine learning and inverse problems because these methods rely on parameter distinguishability. Introducing the "degeneracy distillery," a method estimates and resolves these degenerate parameter combinations both automatically and symbolically using the Fisher information matrix derived from parameter-data pairs. By analyzing the likelihood's information geometry, degeneracies are characterized as intrinsic to the physical model itself, independent of observed data. The technique discovers symbolic coordinate transformations that identify parameters with independent data effects. These resulting coordinates flatten the Fisher information globally in expectation, significantly reducing simulation needs for subsequent posterior estimation compared to point-based methods while providing deeper physical insight.

items.title: Federated Survival Analysis in Healthcare: A Multi-Model Evaluation on Cross-Institutional Heterogeneous Breast Cancer Data
items.summary: Survival analysis demands large, diverse patient datasets, which are often restricted by privacy concerns. Federated learning (FL) offers a solution by training models across multiple institutions without centralizing raw data. This study systematically evaluated three survival models—Cox, DeepSurv, and RSF—using cross-institutional breast cancer data. Results indicated that FL generally outperforms local training and approaches centralized performance; Random Survival Forest demonstrated the best overall balance of robustness. The research also recommended specific federated optimization strategies (FedAvg and FedProx) based on varying client distribution diversity for clinical implementation.

items.title: Stochastic Expectation Maximization for Robust State-Space Radio Interferometric Imaging
items.summary: Proposes a robust estimation scheme for linear state-space models facing compound-Gaussian noise, typical in radio interferometry affected by RFI. The method utilizes Stochastic Approximation Expectation-Maximization (SAEM), modifying the standard E-step with Monte Carlo sampling and Gibbs updates. This approach achieves tractable inference despite the heavy-tailed likelihood imposed by non-Gaussian noise. Numerical experiments demonstrate that this technique substantially enhances reconstruction fidelity compared to Gaussian EM or RTS smoothers. These findings confirm the value of incorporating heavy-tailed state-space modeling for interference-prone imaging applications.

items.title: A Dual Edge Spatial Jacobian Image Graph for Interpretable Diabetic Retinopathy Grading
items.summary: Presents a dual-edge spatial-Jacobian image graph for enhancing the interpretability of diabetic retinopathy (DR) grading from fundus photos. This framework represents each image as a node with four aligned evidence streams: vessel information, lesion maps, contrastive embeddings, and morphometric biomarkers. It models relationships using two edge branches—one encoding vessel-lesion geometry and another detailing embedding-biomarker sensitivity. The combined structure processes data through lightweight attention mechanisms. Tested on 2,910 images, the model achieved strong performance metrics like 0.8312 quadratic weighted kappa for overall grading.

items.title: When Surveys Become Conversations: Adaptive Matrix Validation for AI-Assisted Interviews
items.summary: AI-assisted interviews allow respondents to naturally describe experiences which are then mapped into structured survey variables by an AI system. Because this mapping process is fallible and potentially varies across subgroups, the paper introduces Adaptive Matrix Validation (AMV). AMV requires respondents to complete an interview followed by a small set of randomized structured questions for statistical adjustment. The estimation technique first calibrates the AI-mapped values using validation answers from other participants before correcting errors with the target respondent's specific validation data. The proposed methods provide estimators and planning formulas for item means, subgroup estimates, and regression coefficients derived from these complex interview mappings.

items.title: A Step Towards Inherently Interpretable Causal Machine Learning Models For Decision Support
items.summary: Emphasizing model transparency due to increasing reliance on machine learning in various sectors, this work addresses the limitations of current methods which primarily reveal correlations. It proposes integrating causal machine learning techniques with inherently interpretable models specifically for cross-sectional data. The methodology is evaluated based on both predictive accuracy and interpretability. Results demonstrate that the proposed approach maintains competitive prediction performance while offering structural transparency regarding causal relationships. This contributes to decision support by enabling informed, transparent, and causally grounded "what-if" scenario evaluations.

items.title: Data Augmentation: A Fourier Analysis Perspective
items.summary: Data augmentation exploits known invariances by transforming training samples using a group action. Computational costs restrict using transformations from large groups entirely. This research proposes using Fourier analysis to investigate if sampling only a subset of these group elements retains the statistical benefits of full augmentation. Results demonstrate that for many classical learning problems, partial augmentation achieves the same minimax rates as full augmentation with vanishing error. Conversely, the study proves that achieving exact invariance through data augmentation necessitates averaging over the entire group, precluding use of strict subsets.

items.title: NoLimits.jl: Flexible and Composable Nonlinear Mixed-Effects Modeling in Julia
items.summary: NoLimits.jl is an open-source Julia package designed for flexible and composable nonlinear mixed-effects modeling. Its macro-based language allows users to build observation and latent-state models from diverse components like ODEs, Markov models, and neural networks. The package supports adaptable distributions for both observations and random effects. It offers a unified interface for frequentist inference using methods such as Laplace approximation and SEM, alongside Bayesian MCMC. These features significantly broaden the scope of estimable nonlinear mixed-effects models within one open framework.
